name: Landford
overview: Landford is a village and parish on the northern edge of the New Forest in south-east Wiltshire, about 10 miles south-east of Salisbury, with the parish (which since 2017 also includes Nomansland and Hamptworth) numbering around 1,100 people. Surrounded by heath and woodland grazed by wild New Forest ponies, it is bisected by the A36 and crossed by the River Blackwater. The Grade II listed Church of St Andrew dates from 1858 (incorporating Norman fragments), and Landford Manor is a 17th-century Grade II* listed house.
character: A vibrant, spread-out New Forest-fringe village of woodland and heath, with a strong community and amenities split between Landford and neighbouring Nomansland.
amenities: The Cuckoo Inn and Lamb Inn (Nomansland) nearby
New Forest CofE (VA) Primary School (two sites: Landford and Nomansland)
Recreation grounds at Landford and Nomansland; Nomansland village green
Hamptworth Golf Club with clubhouse and restaurant
Landford Bog and Landford Heath SSSIs

schools.name: The New Forest CofE (VA) Primary School
schools.distance: in village (two sites; Lyndhurst Road, Landford SP5 2AE)

prices.summary: A desirable New Forest-edge village; everything from cottages to substantial houses and a major rural estate, with values supported by the National Park setting and Salisbury grammar catchment. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walking, cycling and riding in the New Forest National Park
Wildlife at Landford Bog and Landford Heath SSSIs
St Andrew's Church and Landford Manor
Nomansland village green and the Lamb Inn
pros: On the edge of the New Forest National Park with heath and woodland
Village shop, Post Office, pub and primary school retained
Good A36/M27 links to Salisbury and Southampton
cons: Spread-out parish; amenities split between Landford and Nomansland
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
whoItSuits: Families wanting a New Forest-fringe village with a primary school, pub and shop, plus Salisbury grammar access and good road links.
primarySupermarket: Village shop and Post Office; supermarkets in Salisbury/Romsey
